title: Next.js dev server allowedDevOrigins bypass: Origin: null skips cross-site validation for internal dev endpoints
description: In blockCrossSite() in packages/next/src/server/lib/router-utils/block-cross-site.ts (the next dev cross-site protection for internal development endpoints, e.g. websocket upgrades for HMR and other privileged dev functionality), the origin allow-list check is gated on `if (rawOrigin && rawOrigin !== 'null')`. When a request carries an Origin header with the literal value 'null' (sent by opaque/privacy-sensitive browser contexts such as sandboxed documents), the condition is false, so the isCsrfOriginAllowed(originLowerCase, allowedOrigins) check is skipped entirely and the function falls through to `return false`, meaning the request is allowed rather than warned/blocked. As a result, even when the developer has configured allowedDevOrigins, attacker-controlled browser code running in an opaque context can connect to internal dev-server endpoints and interact with sensitive dev functionality that should have been blocked. This affects development mode only (next dev), not next start.
